Posted Workers Directive (96/71/EC, revised by 2018/957/EU) — equal pay and working conditions for workers posted abroad within the EU, born from the Bolkestein battles and the 'social dumping' controversy, revised after French and German pressure over wage competition from Central European labour

Pay Transparency Directive (2023/970/EU) — mandatory salary disclosure, pay audits, and gender pay gap reporting obligations for EU employers, the first binding EU instrument targeting the gender pay gap through enforceable transparency obligations

Dublin III Regulation (EU No 604/2013) — which EU member state is responsible for processing an asylum application, the contested system that collapsed under the 2015 refugee crisis and placed disproportionate burden on frontline states Greece and Italy

Asylum Qualification Directive (2011/95/EU) — harmonised EU-wide definition of refugee status and subsidiary protection, replacing the 2004 Qualification Directive after divergent national recognition rates exposed the incoherence of the Common European Asylum System

Air Passenger Rights Regulation (EC 261/2004) — passenger compensation for flight delays, cancellations, and denied boarding, the most-invoked piece of EU consumer law, born after a decade of airline lobbying against voluntary schemes

Council Regulation (EC) No 1/2003 on the implementation of the rules on competition laid down in Articles 81 and 82 of the Treaty — modernised EU antitrust enforcement by ending the old notification system, decentralising enforcement to national competition authorities, and enabling private damages claims

Schengen Borders Code (EU 2016/399) — rules governing the crossing of internal and external Schengen borders, including the conditions for member states to temporarily reintroduce internal border controls, recodified under pressure after the 2015 migration crisis

Orphan Drug Regulation (EC 141/2000) — EU incentives for developing medicines for rare diseases affecting fewer than 5 in 10,000 EU citizens, including ten-year market exclusivity and fee waivers, modelled on the US Orphan Drug Act of 1983

Accounting Directive (2013/34/EU) — annual financial statements and consolidated accounts for EU companies, replacing the Fourth and Seventh Company Law Directives, and introducing country-by-country reporting for multinationals as a tax transparency measure

Frontex Regulation (EU 2019/1896) establishing the European Border and Coast Guard — transformed Frontex from a coordination agency into an operational force with a standing corps of 10,000 officers after the 2015 Schengen crisis and Aegean sea crossings
